summary: Solid soundboard recording of a mixed‑quality show – standout Help > Slip > Frank and early ’93 BIODTL shine despite a weak first set.
review: The Miller SBD capture is generally clear, though levels are low, and fans rave about the ultra‑hot Help > Slip > Frank sequence and the first 1993 BIODTL. The performance swings from a sleepy first set (notably Althea) to a stronger second half with crowd‑pleasing Aiko, pre‑drums China Doll and a beautiful Stella Blue, earning both high praise for Jerry’s leads and some criticism for uneven moments.

ai_rating.rationale: Show quality is mixed (average 3‑star written scores but many enthusiastic comments about key songs) while the soundboard source is clear and well‑recorded; combined weighting yields a solid 4‑star overall rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Clear board mix with low playback levels noted; no major dropouts or distortion, praised as one of the best DATBRAD AUD captures.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set starts promising but quickly drifts into a sleepy, uneven vibe; second set recovers with tighter jams and crowd‑pleasers, culminating in an especially hot Help‑Slip‑Frank sequence.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Multiple reviewers highlight his authoritative leads, perfect tone and "crushes it" solo on the Help>Slip>Frank segment.
band_member_comments.Phil: Praised for amazing bass work that stood out during the show.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keyboards exceeded expectations; Vince (Vinnie) found his spot and delivered a strong outro on the Help>Slip>Frank jam.
band_member_comments.Drums: Drummers described as "awesome" and especially impressive to watch.
